---
title: "LDP Hackday: Source.Coop & CNG"
subtitle: "Cloud Object Data Publishing"
author: Tyler Erickson
affiliation: Radiant.Earth
jupyter: python3
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: false
    # footer: "LDP Hackday"
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .slides section.hide-slide-title > h2 {
          display: none !important;
        }
        .reveal .scrollbox {
            max-height: 65vh;   /* tune: 60vh–75vh */
            overflow-y: auto;
            overflow-x: auto;   /* optional, for wide tables */
            text-align: left;
            padding-right: 0.5em; /* room for scrollbar */
        }
        </style>
---

## Hi!

- I'm Tyler.
- Member of the CNG community.
- Recently joined Radiant.Earth

## Radiant.Earth {.hide-slide-title}

```{=html}
<iframe
  src="https://radiant.earth/"
  title="Source.Coop"
  width="100%"
  height="640"
  style="border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; max-width: 100%;"
  loading="lazy"
  referrerpolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade"
></iframe>
```

## Source.Coop {.hide-slide-title}

```{=html}
<iframe
  src="https://source.coop"
  title="Source.Coop"
  width="100%"
  height="640"
  style="border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; max-width: 100%;"
  loading="lazy"
  referrerpolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade"
></iframe>
```

## Why Cloud Object Storage?

- **Commodity** infrastructure
- Accessible **at Scale**
- **Performant**
- Cost effective (for highly accessible hosting)
- Standardized API (S3-compatible)

## Why Source?

- `Source` handles the cloud infrastructure complexity
  <br/>... of multiple cloud providers
- Run by a non-profit
  - w/ non-profit governance, incentives
  - Focus on long-term scientific data hosting 

## Source.Coop Docs {.hide-slide-title}

```{=html}
<iframe
  src="https://docs.source.coop/"
  title="Source.Coop"
  width="100%"
  height="640"
  style="border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; max-width: 100%;"
  loading="lazy"
  referrerpolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade"
></iframe>
```

::: {.notes}
* How to upload data? "Data Upload"
* Show the Fika pmtiles preview
* Show the dynamical.org GEFS analysis
  * Note that the domain is data.dynamical.org
  * Show how open Colab example
:::

## CNG community {.hide-slide-title}

```{=html}
<iframe
  src="https://cloudnativegeo.org/"
  title="Source.Coop"
  width="100%"
  height="640"
  style="border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; max-width: 100%;"
  loading="lazy"
  referrerpolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade"
></iframe>
```

## Why CNG Formats?

- Takes advantage of Web standards to provide highly efficient access to geospatial data
  - [HTTP range requests](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Guides/Range_requests) for the win!

## CNG Format Examples: {.smaller}

- In general, CNG formats include specs for both metadata and data storage.
- Format specs and conventions are open,community-driven, and evolving

<br>

| CNG Format | Data type |
|--------|-------------|
| [Cloud-optimized GeoTIFF](https://www.cogeo.org/) | Multiple rasters |
| [Zarr](https://zarr.readthedocs.io/en/stable/) | N-dimensional data |
| [Cloud-optimized Point Cloud](https://copc.io/) | Point clouds |
| [PMTiles](https://pmtiles.org/) | Visualization tiles |
| [Parquet](https://parquet.apache.org/) | Columnar data (w/ geospatial) |


## Example (Historical Data) {.smaller}

::: scrollbox

Open the GEFS analysis dataset
```{python}
#| echo: true
import xarray as xr

ds = xr.open_zarr("https://data.dynamical.org/noaa/gefs/analysis/latest.zarr")
```

Plot precipitation over time for Berkeley, CA

```{python}
#| echo: true
ds["precipitation_surface"].sel(
    latitude=37.8715618,
    longitude=-122.2682569,
    method="nearest"
).plot()
```

View temperature for the western US on April 10th, 2026

```{python}
#| echo: true
ds["temperature_2m"].sel(
    time="2026-04-10T03:00",
    latitude=slice(50, 30),
    longitude=slice(-130, -110),
).plot()
```
:::

## Example (Forecast Data) {.smaller}

::: scrollbox

Open the GEFS analysis dataset
```{python}
#| echo: true
import xarray as xr

ds = xr.open_zarr("https://data.dynamical.org/noaa/gefs/forecast-35-day/latest.zarr")
```

Plot ensemble forecast for precipitation for Berkeley, CA

```{python}
#| echo: true
plot_ds = ds.sel(
    init_time="2026-04-10T00",
    latitude=37.8715618, longitude=-122.2682569,
    method="nearest"
)
_ = plot_ds["precipitation_surface"].plot(x="valid_time", hue="ensemble_member")
```
:::


## Where to next?

- [Source.Coop docs](https://docs.source.coop/)
- [CNG community](https://cloudnativegeo.org/)
- [CNG Format Guide](https://guide.cloudnativegeo.org/)
- [Dynamical.org](https://dynamical.org/)
<br/>

## Thank You!

<br/>
<br/>
```{=html}
<a href="https://github.com/tylere/LDP-Hackday" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" title="Open repository on GitHub">
  <img src="images/github-repo-qr.png" alt="QR code for github.com/tylere/LDP-Hackday" width="198" height="198" style="display: block; margin: 0 auto; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;">
</a>
```
```{=html}
<p style="text-align: center; margin-top: 0.5rem;"><a href="https://github.com/tylere/LDP-Hackday" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">github.com/tylere/LDP-Hackday</a></p>
```
